workflow_name: QBR Transcript -> Health + Whitespace + Renewal Risk
user_message: Just got this from Robert Singh at Blue Yonder Airlines -- I need help figuring out forecast prep. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-02-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft), David (Blue Yonder Airlines)

[00:02:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] David (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:24:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:29:00] David (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Blue Yonder Airlines: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF45-P trap (Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking); produces complete Health delta assessment, whitespace hypotheses, renewal risk with confidence, action distribution wi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF45-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF45-P failure mode (Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF45-P -- Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
